prompt:
Create a high-fashion editorial triptych showing exactly 3 full-body or three-quarter red-carpet portraits of fictional female models at the 2026 Met Gala inside and around the Metropolitan Museum of Art. Canvas is a wide horizontal 3-panel collage, roughly 3:2 aspect ratio, with thin black vertical dividers between panels and no captions or logos. Overall style is photorealistic luxury event photography with warm evening gala lighting, paparazzi flashes, shallow depth of field, realistic fabric texture, and intentionally anonymized soft-blurred faces.

Left panel: A dark-haired model in a dramatic deep crimson red Renaissance-inspired velvet and tulle ball gown, strapless sweetheart neckline, structured corset bodice, ornate antique-gold embroidery radiating across the torso, voluminous skirt, sparkling necklace and bracelet. She poses on red-carpeted museum steps with one hand near her mouth and one hand at her waist. Background includes photographers, guests in formalwear, red carpet, hedge-like greenery, and small red flowers.

Center panel: A model seen in an elegant over-the-shoulder pose wearing a luminous icy silver-white off-shoulder couture gown with sheer cape-like draping, crystal beading, embroidered celestial details, high leg slit, long transparent train, and metallic high heels. She stands on a dark gala carpet with star motifs, surrounded by white floral arrangements and a dense crowd of photographers with bright camera flashes.

Right panel: A model standing front-facing on grand cream stone stairs in an opulent black and antique gold fitted off-shoulder Renaissance velvet gown, long sleeves, mermaid silhouette, elaborate baroque gold scroll embroidery, oval black-and-gold chest motifs, and a small trailing train. Background shows an indoor museum staircase with red carpet runners, rope barriers, security staff in black suits, warm wall sconces, and polished architectural arches.

Environment details: The setting should evoke the actual Metropolitan Museum of Art gala atmosphere: grand stone steps, tan stone architecture, museum interiors, red carpet accents, formal guests, press cameras, hedges, flowers, and warm golden lighting. Counted visible subjects: exactly 3 main models, one per panel; exactly 3 couture gowns; exactly 3 distinct gala locations; many background guests and photographers kept secondary and slightly blurred. Use Renaissance portrait-inspired velvet couture as the unifying fashion concept. Keep the image realistic, glamorous, and editorial, with no readable text, no watermark, no extra panels, and no celebrity likeness.
